description: Real source files the pipeline ingests, placed under data/raw/. The two marked status='in_use' are already wired and ingested (all 30 districts, reconciled to NCRB totals). The rest enrich later phases (LGD codes, socio-economic, recent years).
instructions: 1. The two in_use files are fetched into backend/data/raw/ and ingested by `python -m pipeline.run`.
2. To refresh/replace, re-download into data/raw/ keeping the same filename.

fetched_real_sources.ncrb_district_ipc_2013.csv: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/reethified/India-IPC-Crimes-Datasets/master/archived_raw_crimes_by_year/District_wise_crimes_reported_IPC_2013.csv (NCRB district-wise IPC 2013; same 33-column schema, header normalized on ingest). Latest year that reconciles exactly. 2014/2015/2017 use wider sub-split schemas and 2016 omits the state column, so they are not ingested; district-wise IPC is not published machine-readable for 2019+.

datasets.title: District-wise IPC crimes (NCRB via OGD)
datasets.mapping: wide crime-head columns -> canonical category_code via crime_taxonomy.json ncrb_heads; State/District/Year are keys
datasets.notes: District names vary by year; reconcile to geo_units names / aliases.

datasets.title: NCRB Crime in India state/city tables
datasets.mapping: head rows -> category_code; used for state totals & cross-checking district sums
datasets.notes: PDF tables may need tabula/camelot extraction.

datasets.title: Census 2011 district population (Karnataka)
datasets.mapping: district population + female_population -> geo_units (rate denominators); census_district_code -> codes.census_district_code
datasets.notes: Resolves the 'missing LGD/Census code' data-quality warnings and provides real rate denominators.

datasets.title: Socio-Economic Census (Karnataka)
datasets.mapping: socio-economic indicators -> geo_units (feeds risk 'socioeconomic' component, Phase 4)

datasets.title: Karnataka district boundaries (GeoJSON)
datasets.mapping: polygon geometry -> geo_units.boundary_ref + centroid + area_km2 (Phase 2 map layer)
datasets.notes: Phase 2 needs this for the choropleth/heatmap; Phase 1 uses centroids only.
